A1.1.proof: Infrastructure capacity monitoring with automated alerting. Auto-scaling configured for cloud workloads. Capacity planning reviews conducted quarterly with growth projections.

PI1.1.proof: Processing specifications documented for each service. Data definitions and processing rules maintained in technical documentation. Service descriptions provided to customers.

PI1.2.proof: Input validation controls implemented across all customer-facing applications. Completeness checks for batch processing. Error handling procedures documented and tested.

PI1.4.proof: Output delivery controls documented. Report distribution restricted to authorized recipients. Timeliness monitoring for scheduled outputs.

P1.1.proof: Privacy notice published on website and provided during data collection. Notice updated when privacy practices change. Notification procedures documented for material changes.

P1.2.proof: Privacy notice describes data types collected, purposes, retention periods, disclosure practices, and third-party sharing. Reviewed by legal and DPO quarterly.

P2.1.proof: Consent mechanisms implemented for data collection where required. Opt-in/opt-out choices documented and functional. Consent records maintained with timestamps.

P3.1.proof: Data collection limited to stated purposes per privacy notice. Data minimization principle applied in system design. Privacy impact assessments for new collection activities.

P3.2.proof: Explicit consent workflows implemented for sensitive data collection. Consent forms clearly state consequences of withholding consent. Consent obtained prior to collection.

P4.1.proof: Data use limited to purposes identified in privacy notice. Purpose limitation enforced through access controls and data governance policies. Regular audits verify compliance.

P4.2.proof: Retention schedules defined for all personal information categories. Automated retention policies enforce deletion timelines. Retention compliance monitored quarterly.

P4.3.proof: Secure disposal procedures for personal information. Automated purge processes for expired data. Disposal verification through periodic sampling and audit.

P5.1.proof: Data subject access request (DSAR) process documented. Self-service portal for data access. Requests fulfilled within regulatory timelines. Denial procedures with appeal mechanism.

P5.2.proof: Data correction process exists but relies on manual workflows. Third-party notification of corrections not fully automated.
P5.2.plan: Automate data correction workflows and implement automated notification to downstream third-party recipients.

P6.1.proof: Third-party disclosure requires explicit consent where applicable. Consent verified before disclosure. Disclosure procedures documented with legal review.

P6.2.proof: Disclosure log maintained in data governance platform. All authorized disclosures recorded with date, recipient, purpose, and data categories. Quarterly review by DPO.

P6.3.proof: Breach notification procedures documented. Unauthorized disclosure tracking in incident management system. Breach register maintained with investigation outcomes.

P6.4.proof: Data processing agreements with all vendors handling personal information. Privacy requirements in vendor contracts. Annual compliance assessments for critical data processors.

P6.5.proof: Breach notification clauses in vendor contracts. Not all vendors have been assessed for notification capability. Incident response coordination with vendors is informal.
P6.5.plan: Conduct tabletop exercises with critical data processing vendors to validate notification procedures.

P6.6.proof: Breach notification procedures aligned with regulatory requirements (GDPR 72-hour, state breach notification laws). Communication templates prepared. Legal counsel engaged for all notifications.

P6.7.plan: Implement automated data mapping and accounting capability across all personal information repositories.
